title: Enhanced seismic isolation lead rubber bearings
abstract: A lead rubber bearing includes a deformable body (20) comprising rubber layers (22) laminated with metal reinforcement layers (21), and at least one lead plug received in a hole (26) formed through the laminated layers of the deformable body. The deformable body (20) has a thermally conductive interface in contact with the lead plug to provide thermal conduction between the lead plug and at least some of the metal reinforcement layers (21).
claims: Claims 1. A lead rubber bearing, comprising: a deformable body (20) comprising rubber layers (22) laminated with metal reinforcement layers (21); and at least one lead plug (23) received in a hole (26) formed through the laminated layers of the deformable body, wherein the deformable body (20) has a thermally conductive interface in contact with the lead plug (23) to provide thermal conduction between the lead plug and at least some of the metal reinforcement layers (21). 2. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in claim 1, wherein the thermally conductive interface includes at least one thermally conductive deformable portion (33, 34) in contact with at least one of the metal reinforcement layers (21), and exposed at an inner surface of the hole (26) to be in contact with the lead plug (23). 3. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in claim 2, wherein the thermally conductive deformable portion (33, 34) consists of rubber material loaded with thermally conductive particles. 4. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in claim 3, wherein the thermally conductive particles include graphite particles. 5. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in any one of claims 2-4, wherein the thermally conductive deformable portion has: an inner part (33) forming at least part of a wall of the hole (26) receiving the lead plug (23); and radial extensions (34) belonging to the rubber layers (22) of the deformable body (20) laminated with the metal reinforcement layers (21). 6. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in claim 5, wherein the rubber layers (22) of the deformable body (20) include thermally non-conductive portions (32) surrounding the radial extensions (34) of the thermally conductive deformable portion. 7. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in any one of claims 2-6, wherein the thermally conductive deformable portion (33, 34) has a thermal conductivity of more than 0.5 W.rn^ K 1 , and preferably more than 5 W.rn^ K 1 . 8. The lead rubber bearing as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the thermally conductive interface includes edges (28) of the metal reinforcement layers (21) that are exposed at an inner surface of the hole (26) to be in contact with the lead plug (23). 9. A method of manufacturing a lead rubber bearing, the method comprising: stacking rubber layers (22) alternating with metal reinforcement layers (21), wherein the stacked layers have at least one hole (26) therethrough; forming a deformable body (20) with the rubber layers, the metal reinforcement layers and the hole therethrough, wherein forming a deformable body comprises curing the rubber layers; and inserting a lead plug (23) in the hole of the deformable body, wherein the deformable body (20) is formed to have a thermally conductive interface in contact with the lead plug (23) to provide thermal conduction between the lead plug and at least some of the metal reinforcement layers (21). 10. The method as claimed in claim 9, wherein at least some of the stacked rubber layers (22) include: a first ring (31) of rubber material loaded with thermally conductive particles, disposed adjacent to the hole (26); and a second ring (32) of rubber material not loaded with thermally conductive particles, disposed around the first ring. 11. The method as claimed in claim 10, wherein curing the rubber layers (22) is performed by allowing part of the rubber material of the first ring (31) to flow and cover edges of the metal reinforcement layers (21), so as to form a wall of the hole (26) receiving the lead plug (23). 12. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10-11, wherein the thermally conductive particles include graphite particles. 13. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10-12, wherein the rubber material loaded with thermally conductive particles has, after curing, a thermal conductivity of more than 0.5 W.rn^ K 1 , and preferably more than 5 W.rn^ K 1 . 14. The method as claimed in any one of claims 9-13, wherein the thermally conductive interface includes edges (28) of the metal reinforcement layers (21) that are exposed at an inner surface of the hole (26) to be in contact with the lead plug (23). 15. The method as claimed in claim 14, wherein forming the deformable body (20) comprises, after curing the rubber layers (22), removing rubber material at the inner surface of the hole (26) to expose the edges (28) of the metal reinforcement layers (21). 16. The method as claimed in claim 14, wherein forming the deformable body (20) comprises, before curing the rubber layers (22), disposing a removable molding plug in the hole (26), in contact with the edges (28) of the metal reinforcement layers (21).
